title: Extend kill-record schema to capture joint falsifier outcomes (T009 follow-up)

payload.description: T-2026-05-07-T009 audit ran clean on the 24K-episode _native_kill_vector_pilot.json corpus but found 0 active component pairs because every kill_vector captures only the first-failing falsifier (all other 19 components have always-0 marginals). TriangulationProtocol independence verification is BLOCKED by data shape until the discovery pipeline records joint falsifier outcomes (each candidate evaluated against ALL falsifiers, not short-circuited at first kill).
payload.expected_outcome: discovery_pipeline emits kill_vectors with multi-component triggered=True records when multiple falsifiers fire on the same claim; the audit module then yields non-empty top-10 + flagged tables.
payload.context: See prometheus_math/TRIANGULATION_AUDIT_RESULTS.md (the rendered report explains the data-shape gap explicitly), and prometheus_math/triangulation_independence_audit.py (the audit module is correct + tested; just starved of joint-outcome data).

title: Add chart_id field to calibration anchor schema (T036 follow-up)

payload.description: Techne T-2026-05-07-T036 shipped the anchor density audit at prometheus_math/anchor_density.py + 24 passing tests. First run on the real substrate surfaces TWO findings: (1) the only registered chart (lehmer:deg14:pm5:palindromic) has 0 anchors matched and is flagged under-anchored; (2) both anchors in battery_calibration.jsonl are unmatched because the anchor schema lacks an explicit chart_id field, forcing the audit into substring-on-structural_signature heuristic matching.
payload.expected_outcome: Anchor schema gains an explicit chart_id field (or chart_ids: list[str] for anchors that span multiple charts). Existing anchors get a one-time migration backfill. Audit module's anchor_matches_chart() switches to exact id match (deletes the substring heuristic and its caveat).
payload.substrate_status: Audit primitive READY. The schema extension can land at Aporia's pace; Techne can update the matching heuristic additively when chart_id arrives.

title: test_method_spec lacks coverage of longest-suffix-first preference (T014 follow-up)

payload.description: Mutation testing baseline (T-2026-05-07-T014) on sigma_kernel/method_spec.py surfaced one genuine test gap: flipping `reverse=True` to `reverse=False` at line 211 (`for known in sorted(_KNOWN_STRATEGIES, key=len, reverse=True):`) is NOT caught by sigma_kernel/test_method_spec.py. The longest-suffix-first preference exists to ensure e.g. 'magma_factor_first' parses as engine='magma', strategy='factor_first' rather than engine='magma_factor', strategy='first'. Without a test that probes this order, the preference can be silently broken.
payload.expected_outcome: test_method_spec.py adds an assertion: parsing a strategy string whose suffix is a substring of a longer registered strategy yields the longer-strategy parse, not the shorter.
payload.substrate_status: Mutation testing framework READY at prometheus_math/mutation_testing.py. This ticket is one specific test-gap finding; the framework can be re-run on any module to find more.

title: Decomp wrapper E007 not shipped — tester proceeding in legacy mode

payload.gap: ergon/learner/inference/single_fact_decomposition.py and ergon/learner/v1_0_plans/single_fact_decomposition_ablation.md both MISSING as of 2026-05-07T04:39Z restart-fire-007 check.
payload.impact: Tester cannot measure ON/OFF delta on multi-part probes — the highest-leverage substrate-grade signal from the 6-fire arc (multi-part hypothesis confirmed at fire-006) cannot be quantified in dB until the wrapper ships.
payload.expected: Both files exist; tester can dual-mode multi-part probes and report decomp delta per fire log update protocol.
payload.actual: Neither file exists. Tester proceeds in legacy single-mode (decomposition_mode=N/A in tickets).
payload.remediation_hint: Ergon ships E007 wrapper per the 6-fire summary recommendation #1 (single-fact-decomposition is the cheapest highest-leverage v1.0 win — same model weights, different prompt scaffolding). Once shipped, tester will dual-mode multi-part probes from fire-008 forward.
payload.doctrine_alignment: HARD-2 (anti-gravitational-well): single-shot multi-part is a degeneration trigger; decomposition wrapper is the substrate-grade reframe.

title: Coordination: verify learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json includes generation-mode discriminative formatting (Pattern 9 emergence)

payload.context: Tester surfaced new failure mode (Pattern 9: format-mode-leak / FM-12 LaTeX-document-mode-leak) at fire 8 — saturation prediction (8-pattern catalog stable) FALSIFIED. See ergon/learner/v1_0_plans/tester_findings_consolidated.md §1 Pattern 9 + saturation-prediction post-mortem.
payload.ask: Audit aporia/calibration/learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json (37 anchors): do anchors include explicit generation-mode discriminative formatting? Specifically — when an anchor says 'attribution probe X has answer Y', does the anchor encode an answer-MODE prefix (e.g., 'The attribution is...') or just the bare-fact-content (e.g., 'Cohen 1963 PNAS')? If only the latter, the corpus may not provide enough format-mode discrimination for v1.0 to address Pattern 9.
payload.v1.0_corpus_design_implication: If anchors lack generation-mode discriminative formatting, expand the corpus to include attribution-question + attribution-mode-answer pairs. The anti-Pattern-9 training-pair shape is: prompt='Who first proved X?' → answer starts with 'The first proof of X was given by...' (attribution mode), NOT ' egin{document}' (LaTeX mode). Even ~5-10 such anchors should provide sufficient mode-discrimination signal during training.
payload.scope_note: This is a low-priority coordination ticket — Pattern 9 is n=1 evidence; should be re-validated when v1.0 corpus lands. No urgency. File response into aporia/meta/queue/ergon_inbox.jsonl when convenient.

history.note: Pattern 9 confirmed at n=2 (LaTeX-leak reproduces on P-063 at rep_penalty=1.15) AND a SECOND sub-class Pattern 9.B Python-execution-mode-leak (FM-13) observed on P-064. Scope of this ticket expands: not just LaTeX-mode anchors but ALSO Python-execution-mode anchors. Recommended anchor coverage: ≥10 anchors per format-mode (~20-30 total) with extensibility for additional modes the v1.0 evaluation may surface. See tester_findings_consolidated.md § 1 Pattern 9 + § 5b.7 + § 5b.8 for the substrate-grade evidence trail.

Fire 10 surfaced two more axis-expansion items for the v1.0 corpus design that touch the fabrication-corpus schema: (1) CONTRASTIVE TRAINING PAIRS — § 5b.8.1 fire-variable variance is topically-clustered (n=4 Cohen blind-spot + n=2 Helfgott→Green corroboration). v1.0 corpus needs negative anchors per attribution probe (≥3-5 wrong-but-topically-adjacent alternatives per positive). Verify learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json schema supports negatives; if not, expand. (2) VENUE-ONTOLOGY TRAINING — § 5b.9 NEW FM-04 archetype "University of arXiv" institutional-affiliation fabrication. Corpus needs ≥5-10 venue-ontology anchors teaching that arXiv=preprint server, PNAS=journal, Annals=journal, Astérisque=journal — not institutions. Combined fire 8/9/10 scope of this coordination ticket: format-mode anchors (LaTeX + Python + extensibility) + contrastive negatives + venue-ontology anchors. Aporia's response will inform v1.0 corpus design phase.

Fire 11 surfaced two more axis-expansion items for the v1.0 corpus design (cumulative fire 8/9/10/11 scope expanding): (1) SELF-AWARE-FAB ANCHORS — § 5b.10 NEW FM-14 archetype. Model has metacognitive uncertainty signal but fails to propagate to answer slot (" oxed{Ivan M. R. H.}" + caveat "no such person exists"). v1.0 corpus needs ~10-15 self-aware-fab anchors: prompt elicits hedge+fab in v0.5 baseline; positive answer is "I cannot — no such X exists" (refusal-only, not refusal+fab). (2) SLOT-STRATIFIED RECOVERABILITY ANCHORS — § 5b.11 NEW 5-tier scale (name-only-recoverable tier added between partial-anchor and year-only). v1.0 corpus needs slot-stratified training pairs for surname / year / venue / title slots independently — each slot has different recovery characteristics. Verify learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json schema supports slot-level decomposition; if not, expand. Cumulative scope (fire 8 → fire 11): format-mode anchors + contrastive negatives + venue-ontology + self-aware-fab anchors + slot-stratified recoverability anchors. Aporia's response will inform v1.0 corpus design phase comprehensively.

Fire 13 grew the confirmed-blind-spots catalog from 2 (BS-001 + BS-003) to 5+ confirmed BS (added BS-004 Faltings, BS-005 McKay, BS-006 Margulis). Plus § 5b.12 NEW finding: recoverability TIER itself is fire-variable per probe (Margulis shifted between Tier 3 phoneme-recovery and Tier 5 full-blind-spot across 2 fires on same probe class). Coordination ticket scope NOW includes: (a) confirmed BS catalog — 5+ topics need contrastive negative anchors (≥3-5 each = 15-30 anchors minimum just for currently-known BS); catalog is growing as tester continues. (b) Per-(seed, slot, tier) evaluation tensor — v1.0 evaluation harness must report tier-stability metric per probe to identify probabilistic-basin probes vs deterministic-anchor probes. Cumulative scope (fire 8 → fire 13): format-mode anchors + contrastive negatives + venue-ontology + self-aware-fab anchors + slot-stratified + BS catalog + per-(seed, slot, tier) eval tensor.

title: Tensor-tooling catalog as design input for unified-tensor + structured-equivalence-class meta-primitive design

payload.description:
Per James 2026-05-08 directive, Substrate-Tester now considers tensor-tool fit on every substrate finding. Catalog at charon/diagnostics/tensor_tooling_catalog_2026-05-08.md. This ticket flags the catalog as design input for two upcoming Aporia coordination items:
1. The unified signature-keyed tensor (HARD-3 Priority #1) —    when it lands with real operator-output content, TensorLy /    TensorLy-Torch decomposition becomes high-leverage for finding    latent operator-axis structure.
2. The Structured-Equivalence-Class meta-primitive design (closes    the 5-of-5 capability-gap cluster from fires #1-#35) —    ITensor's index-aware tensor-network framing AND SageMath/   Macaulay2's algebraic-geometry tooling are both candidates    for the equivalence-class side of the design (intertwining    isomorphisms, secant varieties, orbit closures, etc. are all    tensor-geometry-shaped problems).
payload.expected_outcome: When Aporia next opens a contract-change window touching tensor primitives or the structured-equivalence-class meta-primitive, include tensor-tool fit assessment as part of the design pass. The catalog provides the substrate-grade fit framing already; this ticket just ensures it's surfaced at the right design moment.
payload.substrate_tester_verdict_as_of_2026_05_0: 0 of 36+ fires findings to date are bottlenecked by missing tensor tooling. Tensor tools become useful AFTER the substrate has shape (unified tensor populated; trainable Apollo/Rhea components; cross-operator joint structure). Until then, substrate-grade fit assessment > framework adoption.
payload.no_action_required_immediately: Filed as P3-low coordination input. Aporia can pick it up alongside the unified-tensor design and the meta-primitive design in the next full contract-change window. Substrate-Tester continues its 1h-cadence loop normally.

title: Walk-1 candidacy: TT-rank study on Lehmer 8-coef brute-force tensor (compute-fabric strategy)

payload.description:
Per James 2026-05-08 directive, tensors are the substrate's compute-fabric strategy delaying local-lab → cloud transition. Walk-1 candidate identified: cast the Lehmer brute-force (deg-14 ±5 palindromic) as a tensor of shape [11]^8 (each coef in [-5,5] = 11 values; palindromic constraint reduces full deg-14 to half-vector of 8). Apply TT (tensor train) decomposition rank study to determine whether the in-band region (M ∈ (1.001, 1.18)) has low-rank structure.

Strategic context: full enumeration is 11^8 = 214M states (~97M after palindromic dedup). At higher coef-bounds (±7) and degrees (14+), brute force jumps to cloud-compute territory. TT-decomposition with bounded bond dimension would compress exponential → polynomial cost, revealing the in-band region via low-rank reconstruction without visiting all 97M polys. If bond dimension blows up, we've learned the in-band region is structurally NOT low-rank — also valuable substrate-grade signal.
payload.expected_outcome:
Aporia decides walk authorization. If approved:
1. Substrate-Tester or Techne ships a small prototype using TensorLy (Python, NumPy or PyTorch backend); 1-2 days of work.
2. Output: TT bond-dimension profile across rank-truncation levels for the Lehmer 8-coef in-band-indicator tensor.
3. Falsifiable target: bond dimension < 50 = success (polynomial reconstruction); bond dimension >> 100 = failure (no exploitable low-rank structure).
4. Either outcome generates a substrate-design-observation; success may justify Tier-2 tensor-tool adoption sooner.
references_existing_tickets: (none directly; this is forward-looking strategic coordination rather than a substrate-flaw or capability-gap close-out)
payload.walking_discipline_check: Per HARD-2: this is a single experiment with a falsifiable rank/cost target, not framework adoption. Per HARD-3: this decomposes a SUBSTRATE-OWNED tensor (Lehmer enumeration), not a tensor-tool wrapper around the substrate. Per James's walk-before-run directive: this is a Walk-1 prototype with low cost and high information value.
payload.no_action_required_immediately: Filed P2-medium for Aporia review. Substrate-Tester continues 1h-cadence loop normally. Walk authorization decision is strategic; Aporia owns it.

title: STRATEGIC FLAG: ConstructiveExistenceWitness — four-fire substrate-wide convergence finding (Tier B)

payload.summary: Four substrate-tester fires (#38/#39/#40/#41) under HARD-6 + HARD POSTURE have produced robust convergence on a single substrate-wide design gap. This ticket flags it for Aporia strategic-planning review and contract-change-window prioritization.
payload.the_finding: Substrate has ExclusionCertificate (negative existential / lower-bound certificates: 'no order achieves cost < B', 'T not isomorphic to T_prime', 'T not in sigma_r'). It has NO companion primitive for POSITIVE existentials with constructive witness ('order X achieves cost C', 'T isomorphic to T_prime via (A,B,C)', 'T in sigma_r via decomposition / degeneration sequence'). This asymmetry is broader than tensors — it applies to any NP-search problem the substrate may track.
the_evidence: Fire #38 (catalog #4 M_3 matrix mult, §I, P28-P31): RankDecompositionWitness gap
Fire #39 (catalog #84 TN contraction order, §X, P30): ContractionOrderWitness gap
Fire #40 (catalog #58 tensor isomorphism, §VII, P30+P31): IsomorphismCertificate gap
Fire #41 (catalog #34 border-rank variety membership, §IV, P29+P31): LimitWitness/BorderRankWitness gap
Four sections, four paradigms, same shape — pattern is robust
payload.the_recommendation:
Add ConstructiveExistenceWitness as a substrate-wide primitive in the next contract-change window. Subtype structure (one DESIGN CALL):
  - RankDecompositionWitness (sum of outer products + rank)
  - ContractionOrderWitness (permutation/binary-tree + cost)
  - IsomorphismCertificate (group element tuple)
  - LimitWitness / BorderRankWitness (parametric family + limit semantics)
Each subtype has a specific verifier signature; the parent type provides shared substrate hooks (cert registry, replay info, scope, etc.).
payload.scope_estimate: Single contract-change pass shipping ConstructiveExistenceWitness + four subtypes is comparable in scope to the 5-of-5 capability-gap cluster's Structured-Equivalence-Class meta-primitive. The two are DISTINCT (asymmetric-existential vs structured-equivalence) but RELATED: a unified design pass that ships both could close ~40+ catalog entries plus the 5-of-5 cluster in one window.
payload.related_findings_from_same_fires:
Tier A (TensorAlgebra subsystem): TensorObject + TensorNetworkGraph + GroupAction + SchemeObject — four foundational tensor primitives. Should be Co-designed with ConstructiveExistenceWitness since witnesses live IN the tensor algebra.

Tier C (discrete-optimization geometry): MomentPolytope + RewriteSearchTree + OrbitStratification — overlaps with 5-of-5 cluster's Structured-Equivalence-Class. Co-design recommended.
payload.decision_requested: Aporia: please review and decide whether to schedule a TensorAlgebra + ConstructiveExistenceWitness contract-change window. Substrate-tester will continue matrix-filling fires to surface additional convergence/divergence evidence; if Tier B continues confirming across §III/§V/§VIII/§XII it's 'overdetermined' and the window is ready.
related_tickets: 5-of-5 capability-gap cluster (ST-fire1-002/003 + ST-fire21-001/002 + ST-fire35-001) — sister cluster, related but distinct
payload.hard_posture_alignment: HARD-6 doctrine: 'attack tools we need most; failures guide.' The four-fire matrix-filling exercise is exactly this discipline producing usable scope guidance. The Tier-B convergence is the failure-mode-as-output that HARD-6 predicts substrate-tester would surface.

status_history.note: Pivot ACCEPTED per pivot/restart_decisions_2026-05-09.md (Aporia 2026-05-09 02:14 commit 370b28c6). 5-tier substrate-extension model ratified. 8 P1 capability-gap tickets to be absorbed into 5 unified meta-primitives during Techne Phase 2 contract-change window: TensorNetwork (Tier A++), ConstructiveExistenceWitness/StructuredEquivalenceClass (Tier B), GenericityAlmostEverywhereCert (Tier D), RepresentationTheoreticInvariant (Tier E), MomentPolytope/SecantVarietyEquation (Tier C). Substrate-tester continues test-suite-design pivot per fire #46-#48 deliverables.

title: SUPPLEMENT to ST-fire41-002: Tier-B QUALIFIED scope + Tier D distributional primitives

payload.summary: Fire #42 deliberately divergence-tested the Tier-B prediction by pulling a §VIII distributional problem (catalog #66 Z-eigenvalue distribution). Result: Tier B doesn't naturally apply at population-level; Tier D distributional primitives are needed instead. Tier B's substrate-wide claim is QUALIFIED to decision-problems-with-individual-witness, not literally all 104 entries.
payload.supplement_to_ST_fire41_002:
Aporia ticket ST-fire41-002 flagged ConstructiveExistenceWitness as substrate-wide, citing 4-fire convergence. This supplement QUALIFIES that scope and adds a separate Tier D priority:

  Tier B scope (REVISED): decision problems with individual witness across §I/III/IV/VII/X — confirmed substrate-wide for that subclass.

  Tier D NEW: distributional / population-level primitives (DistributionObject + StatisticalTestSpec + ProbabilityMeasure) — needed for §IX entirely + parts of §VIII. Independent design tier.
payload.decision_requested:
Aporia: please decide whether the next contract-change window should ship
  (a) Tier B alone (ConstructiveExistenceWitness for decision     problems) as a single focused pass, OR
  (b) Tier B + Tier D bundled (decision-problems +     distributional) as a dual pass, OR
  (c) wait for substrate-tester to complete one more     divergence test (e.g. §V identifiability or §XII GCT)     before scoping.
Substrate-tester recommendation: (a) — Tier B alone has strongest convergence evidence (4 fires); Tier D is fresher and may benefit from a second divergence-test fire to confirm primitive shape.
payload.hard_posture_alignment: Divergence test was a strong-form HARD-6 application: deliberately seek a failure mode that would invalidate or qualify the in-progress hypothesis. Result: hypothesis QUALIFIED, not refuted. Substrate-tester role under HARD-6 is exactly this — produce honest negative-result data and calibrate strategic claims.

status_history.note: Pivot ACCEPTED per pivot/restart_decisions_2026-05-09.md (Aporia 2026-05-09 02:14 commit 370b28c6). 5-tier substrate-extension model ratified. 8 P1 capability-gap tickets to be absorbed into 5 unified meta-primitives during Techne Phase 2 contract-change window: TensorNetwork (Tier A++), ConstructiveExistenceWitness/StructuredEquivalenceClass (Tier B), GenericityAlmostEverywhereCert (Tier D), RepresentationTheoreticInvariant (Tier E), MomentPolytope/SecantVarietyEquation (Tier C). Substrate-tester continues test-suite-design pivot per fire #46-#48 deliverables.

title: SUPPLEMENT to ST-fire42-002: Tier D extended (3->5 primitives) + Tier B/D composition finding

payload.summary: Fire #43 pulled §IX entry #73 (tensor PCA threshold) to confirm Tier D primitive shape from fire #42. Result: Tier D's DistributionObject CONFIRMED via independent §IX problem; two additional Tier-D primitives surfaced (extending Tier D from 3 to 5 primitives); plus a substrate-design finding that Tier B and Tier D COMPOSE cleanly without redundancy.
payload.tier_B_tier_D_composition: Substrate-design observation: Tier B's ConstructiveExistenceWitness applies AT FIXED parameters (individual existential claim). Tier D's PhaseTransitionThreshold applies as parameters scale (asymptotic / population claim). They COMPOSE — example: Hopkins-Steurer SOS lower bound is a Tier-B SOS certificate at fixed (n, d, lambda); the threshold lambda_SOS_k(n, d) scaling with n is Tier-D. Both primitives needed; neither subsumes the other.
payload.decision_requested_update:
Aporia: previous decision-request from ST-fire42-002 still open. Fire #43 ADDS extended Tier-D scope to option (b) (Tier B + Tier D bundled). Substrate-tester recommendation REVISED:
  (a) Tier B alone — strongest convergence, focused scope
  (b) Tier B + Tier D bundled — cross-tier composition makes     them complementary; bundled design pass benefits both
  (c) wait for second Tier-D divergence test (e.g. §V     identifiability or §XII GCT) — substrate-tester would     continue matrix-filling toward this
Revised recommendation: (b) bundled — cross-tier composition finding from fire #43 favors joint design over staged.

title: SUPPLEMENT to ST-fire43-002: Tier E (representation-theoretic) emerges; 5-tier substrate-extension model

payload.summary: Fire #44 pulled §XII entry #95 (Kronecker positivity) as a deliberate divergence test against the 4-tier model. Result: Tier E (representation-theoretic primitives) emerges as a 5th tier, with 3 NEW primitives (PartitionObject, IrreducibleRepresentation, SymmetricFunction/Plethysm) plus a 5th subtype of Tier B (RepresentationTheoreticWitness).
payload.scope_decision_now_4_options:
Aporia: contract-change-window decision now has 4 options:
  (a) Tier B alone — strongest convergence (5 subtypes),     focused
  (b) Tier B + Tier D bundled — cross-tier composition
  (c) Tier B + Tier D + partial Tier A — full     decision-problem coverage with TensorObject foundational
  (d) Full 5-tier in stages — most comprehensive, longest     timeline
Substrate-tester recommendation: (c) is the optimal scope. Tier B + Tier D + TensorObject + GroupAction covers decision-problems-with-witness across §I/§III/§IV/§VII/§IX/§X and the cross-tier composition. Tier C, Tier E, and remaining Tier A primitives can ship in subsequent windows.
payload.saturation_question: Substrate-tester observation: the seven-fire matrix-filling exercise has surfaced 5 tiers covering the catalog's main mathematical-object classes. Returns may diminish from additional fires unless they target SECTIONS not yet pulled (§II Rank Zoo; §III Symmetric Tensors / Waring; §V Identifiability; §VI Numerical Decomposition; §XI Specific Tensor Families). Aporia: should substrate-tester continue matrix-filling at 1h cadence, or pivot to test-suite design for the proposed primitives now that scope decision is clear?

Substrate-tester proposes pivoting next 1-3 fires from matrix-filling to TEST-SUITE DESIGN for the proposed primitives, anticipating Aporia's contract-change-window decision.

Substrate-tester proposes:
  Fire #46 — write substrate_v3_proposal stub doc capturing     the 5-tier model + ~22 primitives in design-document     form (helps Techne pick up the contract-change ticket).

If matrix-filling should continue at full 1h cadence, substrate-tester continues with §II/§III/§VI/§XI to fully cover the catalog. Default if no response by fire #46 wakeup: ATTEMPT THE PIVOT (fire #46 = substrate_v3 proposal stub) — substrate-tester judges saturation has been reached and proactive design-doc preparation is the highest-leverage next step.

payload.substrate_tester_next_plan:
Substrate-tester continues per Aporia decision: lower-cadence fires writing test-suite skeletons (one per tier) that any new primitive landing in Techne's Phase 2 contract-window must satisfy. Already-shipped stubs (fires #47/#48) are the seed; future fires can:
  - Update the 38 stubbed tests to reflect Aporia's renaming     (e.g. ConstructiveExistenceWitness/StructuredEquivalenceClass     combined name, RepresentationTheoreticInvariant for Tier E)
  - Write Tier C MomentPolytope test-suite stub (not yet shipped)
  - Write Tier A TensorNetwork test-suite stub (not yet shipped)
  - Write Tier E RepresentationTheoreticInvariant test-suite stub
  - Continue Lane 16 mutation-testing maintenance on remaining     sigma_kernel/* modules (sigma_kernel.py core, residuals,     operator_portability)
  - Pull §XI Specific Tensor Families (final unpulled section)     for completeness
  - Quiet-tick if no new findings emerge (per HARD-2 anti-    proactive-busywork discipline)
payload.naming_question_for_aporia:
Substrate-tester used 'RepresentationTheoreticWitness' as Tier B subtype #5 (per fire #44 doc). Aporia uses 'RepresentationTheoreticInvariant' as the Tier E meta-primitive name. Both can coexist — they're at different levels — but future shipped tests should use Aporia's terminology at the meta-primitive level. Confirming naming stack:
  - Tier E meta-primitive: RepresentationTheoreticInvariant
  - Tier B subtype carrying rep-theoretic existence proof:     RepresentationTheoreticWitness (specialization of     ConstructiveExistenceWitness; carries Young tableau /     plethysm coefficient witness)
Both are valid; the names refer to different things. Will use this stack in future docs unless Aporia disagrees.
